Description: Configure Actix Web with App and modular service configuration for clean route organization
Do: Use web::scope for route groups; use configure() to split route registration across modules; wrap App with middleware
Dont: Define all routes in main; duplicate scope prefixes

Description: Use web::Data<T> to share application state across handlers (internally Arc-wrapped)
Do: Use web::Data::new() for shared state; access via extractor in handlers; state must be Send + Sync
Dont: Create new connections per request; use global mutable state

Guideline: Use typed extractors for request data
Description: Actix provides extractors for Path, Query, Json, Form, and custom types
Do: Use web::Path<T>, web::Query<T>, web::Json<T> extractors; derive Deserialize for extracted types
Dont: Manually parse request body or URI; extract raw strings and parse them yourself

Description: Define error types that implement actix_web::ResponseError for automatic HTTP error responses
Do: Map domain errors to HTTP status codes; log internal errors server-side; return JSON error bodies
Dont: Expose internal errors to clients; return plain text errors

Description: Attach middleware with App::wrap() or Scope::wrap() for cross-cutting concerns
Do: Use actix_web::middleware::Logger for access logs; use custom Transform for auth; use wrap_fn for simple middleware
Dont: Write middleware from scratch for standard needs; skip logging

Description: Actix guards provide additional route matching beyond path (headers, methods, content type)
Do: Use guard::Header for content negotiation; guard::fn_guard for custom matching; combine guards with .guard()
Dont: Implement content negotiation manually in handlers

Description: Actix provides test utilities for testing handlers and services without starting a server
Do: Use test::init_service and test::TestRequest; test both success and error responses
Dont: Start a real server for unit tests; skip error path testing

Description: Actix-web spawns one worker per CPU core by default; tune based on workload
Do: Set workers based on CPU cores and workload type; use keep_alive for connection management
Dont: Accept default worker count without considering workload
Code Bad: HttpServer::new(|| App::new())\n    .workers(1) // only one worker, wastes CPU cores\n    .bind("0.0.0.0:8080")?

Description: Use actix-web-actors for WebSocket connections with the Actor model
Do: Define a WebSocket actor; implement StreamHandler for messages; handle ping/pong and timeouts
Dont: Keep connections alive without heartbeat; block inside message handlers

Description: Use HttpResponse::streaming() for large responses or Server-Sent Events
Do: Use streaming for file downloads and SSE; set Content-Type appropriately; handle backpressure
Dont: Load entire file into memory before sending
